text.title: Collaborative Multi-Agent Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for shipping a collaborative multi-agent loop that does not bias, inflate, or silently overspend.

rule.statement: The synthesis step MUST receive ALL contributor outputs in one call, not iteratively layered. Never feed one contribution first, ask for a draft, then layer the rest.
rule.rationale: Iterative merging anchors the synthesis on whichever contribution arrived first regardless of its quality; simultaneous injection gives each contribution equal weight.

rule.statement: `max_iterations` MUST be set explicitly (default 5, hard ceiling 10). A budget guard MUST run after every iteration; if used_tokens ≥ 0.9 × budget.total_tokens the loop aborts and proceeds to synthesis with what is already collected.
rule.rationale: Collaborative is the most expensive pattern (N×M LLM calls). Without explicit cap and budget guard, runaway cost is the default failure mode.

rule.statement: The synthesizer agent MUST be a separate identity from all contributors; ideally on a different model family (e.g., contributors on `sonnet`, synthesizer on `opus` or GPT-class).
rule.rationale: Using a contributor as synthesizer over-weights its own contribution; using a different model family breaks the echo-chamber convergence common to same-family runs.

rule.statement: The shared workspace MUST be append-only — every entry recorded with `{agent, iteration, content, ts, schema_version}`. Previous entries are never edited or deleted.
rule.rationale: Append-only gives a deterministic audit trail; mutation makes the contribution history non-reproducible and defeats record-replay debugging.

rule.statement: Per-iteration prompt to each contributor MUST include only the LATEST contribution from each OTHER agent (not the full history) plus this agent's own latest. Capped at ~3000 tokens of other-agent context per iteration.
rule.rationale: Full history grows linearly; iteration N pays for N copies of every contribution. Latest-only keeps the prompt flat without losing the up-to-date state.
